questions.question: Who is often referred to as "the sweet psalmist of Israel"?
questions.answer: David

questions.question: What is the shortest Psalm in the Bible?
questions.answer: Psalm 117

questions.question: What is the longest Psalm, and what is its primary focus?
questions.answer: Psalm 119 (Word of God)

questions.question: Which Psalm begins with the words, "The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want"?
questions.answer: Psalm 23

questions.question: According to Psalm 1, what does the blessed man "delight" in?
questions.answer: The Law of the LORD

questions.question: What does the Hebrew word "Selah," frequently found in the Psalms, most likely signify?
questions.answer: A pause or musical interlude

questions.question: Which prophetess sang a song after crossing the Red Sea, and in which book is it found?
questions.answer: Miriam (Exodus)

questions.question: Besides Ephesians, what other New Testament letter gives instruction about the components of singing (psalms, hymns, spiritual songs)?
questions.answer: Colossians

questions.question: What title or superscription is found above many Psalms indicating David's authorship?
questions.answer: A Psalm of David

questions.question: According to Psalm 95:1-2, what should we do as we come into God's presence?
options: Come with thanksgiving and joyful songs
questions.answer: Come with thanksgiving and joyful songs

questions.question: Which Psalm concludes with the phrase, "Let everything that has breath praise the LORD. Praise the LORD"?
questions.answer: Psalm 150

questions.question: Which biblical character sings a song known as the "Magnificat" in Luke 1?
questions.answer: Mary

questions.question: What Psalm is traditionally viewed as David's great prayer of repentance written after Nathan confronted him about his sin?
questions.answer: Psalm 51

questions.question: Which Psalm describes the heavens declaring the glory of God and the firmament showing His handiwork?
questions.answer: Psalm 19

questions.question: How is the Book of Psalms structurally divided?
options: Into 7 sections based on the days of the week
Into 12 sections based on the tribes
questions.answer: Into five "books"

questions.question: Name one of the collections of songs found within the Psalter, often sung by pilgrims.
questions.answer: Songs of Ascents

questions.question: What does Psalm 103 instruct the soul to do before listing God's benefits?
questions.answer: Bless the LORD

questions.question: What does the term "Maskil" in the heading of some Davidic Psalms generally signify?
questions.answer: An instructional or contemplation poem

questions.question: Which Psalm is famous for beginning with the question, "How long, O LORD? Will you forget me forever?"
questions.answer: Psalm 13

questions.question: What historical event in Israel is the context for the "Song of Moses" in Deuteronomy 32?
options: The crossing of the Red Sea
A farewell address before his death
questions.answer: A farewell address before his death

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Greek word for "hymns" mentioned in Colossians 3:16?
options: A song of praise addressed directly to God
questions.answer: A song of praise addressed directly to God

questions.question: Who is a major author of Psalms, besides David, often associated with the Temple music?
questions.answer: Asaph

questions.question: Which Psalm begins with, "God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble"?
questions.answer: Psalm 46

questions.question: Which Psalm prophesies the Messiah would be forsaken by God with the cry, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"?
questions.answer: Psalm 22

questions.question: What is the significance of the Psalms being used as liturgy in the Jerusalem Temple?
options: They were private prayers for the priests
They were structured texts for public worship
They were only sung during annual festivals
They were secret teachings of the Levites
questions.answer: They were structured texts for public worship

questions.question: What was Michal's reaction when David danced and rejoiced greatly before the Ark of the Covenant?
options: She joined him in the street
She despised him in her heart
questions.answer: She despised him in her heart

questions.question: Which Psalm is often referred to as the "Creation Psalm," praising God for His governance over nature and animals?
questions.answer: Psalm 104

questions.question: Which Psalm contains the phrase "For his mercy endures forever" in nearly every verse?
questions.answer: Psalm 136

questions.question: How does Psalm 137 describe the mood of the Israelites in exile, concerning singing and music?
options: They composed new songs of triumph
They hung up their lyres and refused to sing
They sang the songs quietly in private
questions.answer: They hung up their lyres and refused to sing

questions.question: Which New Testament figure sings a song of praise after his voice was restored (father of John the Baptist)?
questions.answer: Zechariah

questions.question: What is man commanded to do in Psalm 46:10 before being told, "Be still, and know that I am God"?
questions.answer: Be still (cease striving)

questions.question: Which Psalm is titled the "Prayer of Moses, the man of God"?
questions.answer: Psalm 90

questions.question: Which King of Judah restored the Temple service and appointed Levites to praise God with instruments after a period of desecration?
questions.answer: Hezekiah

questions.question: Which Psalm contains the famous verse, "Hide me under the shadow of your wings"?
questions.answer: Psalm 17

questions.question: In what context did David first use his harp in King Saul's palace?
questions.answer: To soothe Saul's troubled spirit

questions.question: What does the term "Miktam" in the title of some Davidic Psalms suggest?
options: A song to be sung quietly
questions.answer: A "golden" or precious Psalm

questions.question: Which Psalm declares that "a day in your courts is better than a thousand elsewhere"?
questions.answer: Psalm 84

questions.question: To whom are many of the Psalms of Ascent attributed?
questions.answer: David and Solomon

questions.question: Who said: "I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have being"?
questions.answer: David

questions.question: Which Psalm encourages praising God with a "new song"?
questions.answer: Psalm 96

questions.question: What type of song did Jesus and his disciples sing after the Last Supper before going to the Mount of Olives?
questions.answer: A Hallel Psalm

questions.question: Which Psalm describes the blessedness of the forgiveness of sin?
questions.answer: Psalm 32

questions.question: To whom is Psalm 127 primarily attributed, stating that "unless the LORD builds the house, those who build it labor in vain"?
questions.answer: Solomon

questions.question: How does Psalm 119 describe God's word in relation to one's feet and path?
options: A lamp to my feet and a light to my path
questions.answer: A lamp to my feet and a light to my path

questions.question: What does God do with the righteous, according to Zephaniah 3:17?
options: He quiets them with His love
He rejoices over them with singing
questions.answer: He rejoices over them with singing

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase "Hal-le-lu-yah"?
questions.answer: Praise Yah (Praise the LORD)

questions.question: What is the significance of instructions like "To the tune of The Lilies" in some Psalm headings?
options: They are ancient Hebrew names for the instruments
They refer to the Temple decorations
They are musical notations indicating the melody
questions.answer: They are musical notations indicating the melody

questions.question: Which Psalm is a corporate call to praise beginning, "Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God"?
questions.answer: Psalm 147

questions.question: What is the title of the collection of songs compiled by King David that were specifically for public worship?
questions.answer: The Book of Psalms

questions.question: Which prophetess, besides Miriam, is noted for her song of thanksgiving after a great military victory?
questions.answer: Deborah

questions.question: What musical instrument did Gideon use, besides the torch, to defeat the Midianites?
questions.answer: The Trumpet (Shofar)

questions.question: What is the name of the first musician mentioned in the Bible, a descendant of Cain?
questions.answer: Jubal

questions.question: What musical instruments were played exclusively by the priests, not the Levites, in the Temple?
questions.answer: The Silver Trumpets

questions.question: What instrument was part of Nebuchadnezzar's band that accompanied the decree to worship his golden statue (Daniel 3)?
questions.answer: Horn

questions.question: With what instrument is God commanded to be praised "in His mighty firmament" in Psalm 150?
questions.answer: Loud-sounding cymbals

questions.question: What was the name of the most common lyre or stringed instrument used in the ancient Jewish Temple?
questions.answer: Kinnor

questions.question: What instrument had to sound at sunset on the Day of Atonement (Yom Kippur) to proclaim the Year of Jubilee?
questions.answer: The Shofar (Ram's Horn)

questions.question: What was the main purpose of the shofar or ram's horn in the life of Israel?
options: To signal alarms, assemblies, and coronations
questions.answer: To signal alarms, assemblies, and coronations

questions.question: Which prophet describes a "Song of the Vineyard" to illustrate Israel's relationship with God?
questions.answer: Isaiah

questions.question: What two types of "cymbals" does Psalm 150 mention for praising God?
questions.answer: Loud-sounding and high-sounding/clashing

questions.question: Which prophet condemned the use of the harp, lyre, tambourine, and flute at feasts that ignored God's work?
questions.answer: Isaiah (5:12)

questions.question: Which Bible passage compares speaking in tongues without love to a "clanging cymbal or a noisy gong"?
questions.answer: 1 Corinthians 13:1

questions.question: What instrument, besides the trumpet, did the Levites play during the dedication of Solomon's Temple?
questions.answer: Cymbals, Harps, and Lyres

questions.question: What instruments did Joshua command to be sounded when the walls of Jericho fell?
questions.answer: Shofars (Ram's horns)

questions.question: What was the favorite instrument of Miriam, and what did she do with it in Exodus 15?
questions.answer: Tambourine (danced and led women)

questions.question: What type of instrument is associated with the announcement of Christ's second coming and the resurrection of the dead?
questions.answer: Trumpet

questions.question: What does the term "psaltery" refer to in the biblical musical context?
options: A wind instrument like a flute
A stringed instrument similar to a lyre or lute
questions.answer: A stringed instrument similar to a lyre or lute

questions.question: Which King of Judah, besides David, restored the use of "David's instruments" for worship?
questions.answer: Hezekiah

questions.question: What type of sound was forbidden in the construction of Solomon's Temple?
options: The sound of hammers or iron tools
questions.answer: The sound of hammers or iron tools

questions.question: Which instrument is mentioned in Psalm 33:2 as having ten strings?
questions.answer: The Ten-stringed Lyre (Asor)

questions.question: Which passage in Revelation refers to the 24 elders in heaven holding "harps" and golden bowls?
questions.answer: Revelation 5:8

questions.question: What instruments did the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun primarily play under David's direction?
questions.answer: Cymbals, harps, and lyres

questions.question: What instrument was used in the Temple to mark the entrance and exit of the priests during their service?
questions.answer: Silver Trumpets

questions.question: Which biblical character was scolded by Laban for not allowing him to send him off with songs and music?
questions.answer: Jacob

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew term "nebel," often translated as psaltery or lyre?
questions.answer: A stringed instrument (harp or lute)

questions.question: What warning did God give Israel about the sound of their music because of their disobedience?
options: He would not listen to the melody of their harps (Amos 5:23)
He would replace their instruments with foreign ones
questions.answer: He would not listen to the melody of their harps (Amos 5:23)

questions.question: What was one context for the use of the silver trumpets made by Moses?
options: Calling the congregation or directing the camps' journeys
questions.answer: Calling the congregation or directing the camps' journeys

questions.question: What instruments were played by the company of prophets in 1 Samuel 10:5 as they came down from the high place?
questions.answer: Harp, tambourine, flute, and lyre

questions.question: What type of song is the "Lament of David for Saul and Jonathan"?
questions.answer: A Dirge or funeral song

questions.question: Which instrument was common in feasts and banquets, often associated with joy?
questions.answer: The Flute and Tambourine

questions.question: What does "cymbals of loud clanging" mean in Psalm 150?
options: Cymbals used by the priests only
questions.answer: Cymbals of triumphant joy (loud sound)

questions.question: What does the book of Nehemiah mention the Levites wore while playing their instruments during a great celebration?
questions.answer: Fine linen

questions.question: What are the "pipes" or "flutes" mentioned in Psalm 150?
options: Wind instruments (like the halil or ugab)
Stringed instruments played with a bow
questions.answer: Wind instruments (like the halil or ugab)

questions.question: What warning does Amos 5:23 give about the sound of Israel's songs and harps?
options: God will not listen to them because of hypocrisy
questions.answer: God will not listen to them because of hypocrisy

questions.question: What instrument was used to announce the anointing of Solomon as king?
questions.answer: Trumpet/Shofar

questions.question: In the book of Job, which musical action did Job take after suffering loss, indicating profound grief?
options: Tore his robe and fell to the ground
questions.answer: Tore his robe and fell to the ground

questions.question: What does the "tambourine" or "timbrel" signify in the context of biblical dancing and praise?
questions.answer: Triumphal joy and celebration

questions.question: Which prophet describes the sound of the cherubim's wings as the "noise of great waters" (powerful sound)?
questions.answer: Ezekiel

questions.question: What is the significance of the "Song of the Well" in Numbers 21?
options: It was a spontaneous song of gratitude after God provided water
It was a song sung during a drought
It was a lament for lost resources
It was a prophetic song about Christ
questions.answer: It was a spontaneous song of gratitude after God provided water

questions.question: What does Revelation 14:3 describe as being sung "before the throne and before the four living creatures and the elders"?
questions.answer: A new song

questions.question: Which instrument is associated with dancing in Psalm 149, commanding the saints to praise God?
questions.answer: Tambourine (Timbrel)

questions.question: What is the significance of the phrase "At the sound" in Nebuchadnezzar's decree?
options: It marked the start of the feast
It was the signal to prostrate and worship the image
It was the signal for the army to march
questions.answer: It was the signal to prostrate and worship the image

questions.question: Which prophet of Israel describes the people singing songs on "the night of a solemn feast" when the Passover is celebrated?
questions.answer: Isaiah

questions.question: What instrument was used by the watchmen to give alarm on the city walls?
questions.answer: The Trumpet (Shofar)

questions.question: What instruments were used for great joy during the Feast of Tabernacles, one of the three great annual feasts?
questions.answer: All instruments

questions.question: What musical instrument is specifically mentioned in 1 Samuel 16:23 that David played to calm Saul?
questions.answer: The Harp (Lyre)

questions.question: What is the implication of using "stringed instruments and flutes" together in biblical praise?
options: That only these two types of instruments existed
A rich and diverse orchestration (melody and harmony)
questions.answer: A rich and diverse orchestration (melody and harmony)

questions.question: Which King of Judah used singers and a choir marching ahead of the army that led to a great victory without battle?
questions.answer: Jehoshaphat

questions.question: What type of literature is the book of Song of Solomon (or Song of Songs)?
questions.answer: Love songs and bridal descriptions

questions.question: What does the sound of the trumpets, often seven of them, symbolize in the book of Revelation?
options: Divine judgment and the announcement of events
questions.answer: Divine judgment and the announcement of events

questions.question: What does Jesus tell the Samaritan woman is the correct way to worship God?
options: By following the Law of Moses
questions.answer: In spirit and in truth

questions.question: According to John 4:24, what theological reason does Jesus give for why God must be worshipped in spirit and truth?
questions.answer: Because God is Spirit

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew word for "Praise" that involves extending the hands?
questions.answer: Yadah

questions.question: What is the original meaning of the Greek word for "worship" (proskuneo)?
options: To kiss the hand toward someone (prostrate)
questions.answer: To kiss the hand toward someone (prostrate)

questions.question: What does Paul mean by "worshipping with understanding" in 1 Corinthians 14:15?
options: Praying/singing with both the spirit and the mind (intelligence)
questions.answer: Praying/singing with both the spirit and the mind (intelligence)

questions.question: What was the first recorded act of worship in the Bible performed by Cain and Abel?
questions.answer: Offering sacrifices

questions.question: Which biblical character sings a prayer of thanksgiving from the belly of a fish?
questions.answer: Jonah

questions.question: Why did David bring the Ark of the Covenant to Jerusalem with great rejoicing and songs?
options: To establish Jerusalem as the center of worship
questions.answer: To establish Jerusalem as the center of worship

questions.question: What instruction does Paul give the church in Ephesians 5:19 about being filled with the Spirit in relation to singing?
options: Speaking to one another in psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs
questions.answer: Speaking to one another in psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs

questions.question: What form of worship did the early church practice, according to Acts 2:42?
options: Teaching, fellowship, breaking of bread, and prayers
Singing only the Old Testament Psalms
questions.answer: Teaching, fellowship, breaking of bread, and prayers

questions.question: Which prophet sings a song in exile that praises God for His mercy, despite the affliction, saying His compassions never fail?
questions.answer: Jeremiah (in Lamentations)

questions.question: What did King Jehoshaphat do, instead of putting his army first, that led to victory against his enemies?
options: He appointed singers to go out before the army
questions.answer: He appointed singers to go out before the army

questions.question: What biblical characters were singing in prison when an earthquake occurred, opening the doors?
questions.answer: Paul and Silas

questions.question: What is the primary focus of the "worship" performed by the four living creatures in Revelation?
options: Great and Marvelous are Your Works
questions.answer: Holy, Holy, Holy

questions.question: Where in the Bible is the command given to observe a "Feast of Ingathering" (Feast of Tabernacles) with joy?
questions.answer: Exodus 23:16

questions.question: What did the people do with their voices when the foundation of the second Temple was laid (Ezra 3)?
options: They sang responsively and shouted with a great shout of joy
questions.answer: They sang responsively and shouted with a great shout of joy

questions.question: In the parable of the Prodigal Son, what signaled to the older brother that there was a celebration at home?
options: The sound of music and dancing
questions.answer: The sound of music and dancing

questions.question: What instruction did God give to the Levites regarding their role in the Temple music ministry?
options: They were to serve only one year
They were to play only the trumpet
They were set apart for the service of the song
They were to lead worship only on the Sabbath
questions.answer: They were set apart for the service of the song

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the "silence in heaven" in Revelation 8?
options: It was a sign of eternal rest
It was the end of all singing
It occurred for about half an hour before the judgment trumpets
It was a sign of the saints' prayers
questions.answer: It occurred for about half an hour before the judgment trumpets

questions.question: What prophetic action did the prophet Elisha require before he could receive the word of the LORD (2 Kings 3)?
questions.answer: Bring me a musician

questions.question: Where in the Old Testament is a person commanded to "sing aloud in the night" about God's love and command?
questions.answer: Psalm 42:8

questions.question: What is the spiritual significance of the "new song" in the book of Revelation?
options: A song written by the elders
Redemption and praise for God's saving work through the Lamb
questions.answer: Redemption and praise for God's saving work through the Lamb

questions.question: How did the worshippers in the time of Ezra and Nehemiah express their deep repentance during their solemn assemblies?
options: Fasting, wearing sackcloth, and confessing their sins
questions.answer: Fasting, wearing sackcloth, and confessing their sins

questions.question: What does Psalm 149 say is the purpose of the high praises of God being in the mouth of the saints?
options: To execute vengeance and judgment upon the nations
questions.answer: To execute vengeance and judgment upon the nations

questions.question: What did the prophet Amos condemn about the Israelites' religious festivals and solemn assemblies?
options: He rejected them because they lacked justice and righteousness
questions.answer: He rejected them because they lacked justice and righteousness

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the "sound of a voice speaking" on Mount Sinai during the giving of the Law?
options: The people saw the form of a man
The people heard the sound of words but saw no form
The voice was only heard by Moses
It sounded like a gentle whisper
questions.answer: The people heard the sound of words but saw no form

questions.question: What were the Levite singers' duties during the daily offerings in the Temple?
options: To stand guard at the gates
To praise the LORD with instruments
questions.answer: To praise the LORD with instruments

questions.question: What event marks the first recorded use of the word "worship" in the Bible (in English translations)?
options: Abraham telling his servants he and Isaac will worship
Adam and Eve hiding from God
questions.answer: Abraham telling his servants he and Isaac will worship

questions.question: In which book of the Bible is the command, "Let everything that has breath praise the LORD," found?
questions.answer: Psalms

questions.question: What spiritual state does James 5:13 recommend for someone who is cheerful?
questions.answer: Let him sing psalms (or praise)

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew word "yadah" often used for giving thanks or praise?
options: To throw, cast, or praise with extended hands
questions.answer: To throw, cast, or praise with extended hands

questions.question: What is the focus of Praise in biblical terms (in contrast to Worship)?
options: Focuses on what God has done
questions.answer: Focuses on what God has done

questions.question: What did the multitude of the heavenly host sing at the birth of Jesus (Luke 2)?
options: Glory to God in the highest...
Hallelujah, for the Lord God reigns
questions.answer: Glory to God in the highest...

questions.question: What does it mean for the Word of Christ to "dwell richly" in believers, in relation to singing (Colossians 3:16)?
options: The content of our songs should be rich in scriptural truth
questions.answer: The content of our songs should be rich in scriptural truth

questions.question: What did the people do with their bodies during intense worship or repentance, besides singing or crying out?
options: Prostrate themselves or tearing their garments
questions.answer: Prostrate themselves or tearing their garments

questions.question: What is the main theme of the "Song of Hannah" in 1 Samuel 2?
options: God exalts the humble and humbles the proud
questions.answer: God exalts the humble and humbles the proud

questions.question: What does the Psalmist say about the mouths of the dead in relation to praising the LORD?
options: They will one day be resurrected to praise
The dead do not praise the LORD
questions.answer: The dead do not praise the LORD

questions.question: What does the New Testament teach about singing "spiritual songs"?
options: They are songs written by the apostles
They must be sung in tongues
They are songs inspired by the Spirit or focused on spiritual truths
They must be sung in groups of three
questions.answer: They are songs inspired by the Spirit or focused on spiritual truths

questions.question: What is the significance of the singers and musicians being dressed in fine linen in the Temple service?
options: It symbolizes purity and the sanctity of their service
It was commanded by King David
questions.answer: It symbolizes purity and the sanctity of their service

questions.question: What did the prophet Habakkuk vow to do, even if the fig tree did not blossom and the field yielded no food?
options: Rejoice in the LORD and joy in the God of his salvation
questions.answer: Rejoice in the LORD and joy in the God of his salvation

questions.question: What is the significance of the "Song of Deborah and Barak" in the Book of Judges?
options: It is a prayer for mercy
It is a hymn of victory celebrating God's power
questions.answer: It is a hymn of victory celebrating God's power

questions.question: What does God say about the "sound of revelry" and worship of those who are far from Him?
questions.answer: He hates and rejects it (Amos 5:21)

questions.question: What phrase did the priests and Levites repeat after the offering was made during the dedication of the Second Temple?
options: For He is good, for His mercy endures forever
questions.answer: For He is good, for His mercy endures forever

questions.question: What is the primary command concerning singing in a believer's heart, according to Ephesians 5:19?
options: Making melody in your heart to the Lord
questions.answer: Making melody in your heart to the Lord

questions.question: What did King Hezekiah do to restore the Temple worship and music after the period of desecration?
options: Cleansed the Temple and re-established the Levitical service
Moved the Ark to his palace
questions.answer: Cleansed the Temple and re-established the Levitical service

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew word "zamar," often translated as "sing praise"?
options: To pluck or strike the strings of an instrument (sing with accompaniment)
questions.answer: To pluck or strike the strings of an instrument (sing with accompaniment)

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the "sound of a trumpet" from heaven in the book of Exodus (Sinai)?
options: It was heard only by Moses
The sound was loud and increasingly strong
questions.answer: The sound was loud and increasingly strong

questions.question: What is the significance of the "Song of the Lamb" in Revelation 15?
options: A song of mourning for sin
A song of victory and vindication for God's judgments
A song about the creation of the world
questions.answer: A song of victory and vindication for God's judgments

questions.question: In the Temple context, what was the function of the "libation offering" in relation to the singing of the Psalms?
options: The singing often accompanied the pouring of the libation
It was a sign of judgment
It was only done in the Tabernacle
questions.answer: The singing often accompanied the pouring of the libation

questions.question: What is the connection between singing and the Holy Spirit in the New Testament epistles?
options: Singing is an evidence of being filled with the Spirit
The Spirit only allows silent prayer
The Spirit guides the choice of instruments
Only apostles could be guided by the Spirit in singing
questions.answer: Singing is an evidence of being filled with the Spirit

questions.question: Who was the chief of the three heads of the Levitical singers during David's reign (besides Heman and Jeduthun)?
questions.answer: Asaph

questions.question: What theological point does the "Canzone" (song) in the book of Isaiah make about God's great name?
questions.answer: His name is "Wonderful" (Isaiah 25:1)

questions.question: What did King David dedicate to the LORD for the maintenance of the Temple singers and musicians?
options: Gold and silver from the spoils of war
questions.answer: Gold and silver from the spoils of war

questions.question: Why is music often associated with the prophetic ministry in the Old Testament (e.g., Elisha)?
options: To calm the mind and prepare the prophet to receive God's word
questions.answer: To calm the mind and prepare the prophet to receive God's word

questions.question: What key difference existed between the Levitical musicians' role in the Tabernacle and their role in the Temple?
options: They only sang in the Tabernacle
The Tabernacle had no fixed organization; the Temple had a permanent choir
They only played instruments in the Tabernacle
They were only gatekeepers in the Tabernacle
questions.answer: The Tabernacle had no fixed organization; the Temple had a permanent choir

questions.question: What does the Bible teach about the motives behind music and worship?
options: That it should be aesthetically pleasing
It must be done with a sincere heart and for the glory of God
questions.answer: It must be done with a sincere heart and for the glory of God

questions.question: Besides Miriam and Deborah, which third prominent biblical woman is noted for her song of thanksgiving?
questions.answer: Hannah

questions.question: What specific instruction did David give concerning the use of the stringed instruments when bringing the Ark to Jerusalem?
options: They should be played by the priests only
They were appointed to sound forth with joy
They should be played in a minor key
questions.answer: They were appointed to sound forth with joy

questions.question: How did the High Priest use the bells on his robe in connection with his ministry?
options: They called the people to worship
Their sound ensured the people knew he was alive and ministering before God
They helped him keep time for the singers
questions.answer: Their sound ensured the people knew he was alive and ministering before God

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the "songs of Zion" in a foreign land (Psalm 137)?
options: They became more popular in exile
They were translated into the Babylonian language
The exiles refused to sing them for their captors
questions.answer: The exiles refused to sing them for their captors

questions.question: What principle of worship does the New Testament establish regarding the use of instrumental music in the church?
options: Mandates the use of the organ
Emphasis is on vocal music and singing in the heart; instruments are not mandated
questions.answer: Emphasis is on vocal music and singing in the heart; instruments are not mandated

questions.question: What does Paul imply about the melody in the heart when singing to the Lord?
options: The external sound is all that matters
The focus is the inner devotion and heart's attitude
The melody must be the same for all
questions.answer: The focus is the inner devotion and heart's attitude

questions.question: Who was responsible for setting up the music and service of the Temple, including the gatekeepers and treasuries?
questions.answer: King David

questions.question: What does the book of Job say about the singing and shouting of the morning stars?
options: They sang together when the foundations of the earth were laid
questions.answer: They sang together when the foundations of the earth were laid

questions.question: Why did David choose the Levites for the ministry of music?
options: God had set apart the Levites for the service of the Tabernacle/Temple
They petitioned him for the role
questions.answer: God had set apart the Levites for the service of the Tabernacle/Temple

questions.question: What is the connection between the Ark of the Covenant and the establishment of the worship ministry?
options: The Ark's arrival in Jerusalem prompted David to organize a full-time musical ministry
Music was forbidden when the Ark was present
The Ark was used as a stage
The singers were carried on the Ark
questions.answer: The Ark's arrival in Jerusalem prompted David to organize a full-time musical ministry

questions.question: What does the Apostle Peter encourage believers to do with their voices as "living stones"?
options: Offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God
questions.answer: Offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God

questions.question: What is the significance of the frequent mention of "choir masters" or "chief musicians" in the Psalms?
options: It shows David was a great conductor
It meant the music was simple
It highlights the professional and disciplined nature of Temple worship
It was a common military title
questions.answer: It highlights the professional and disciplined nature of Temple worship

questions.question: What Old Testament prophet describes the earth singing and shouting for joy because of God's redemption?
questions.answer: Isaiah

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the song of the redeemed in heaven (Revelation 14)?
options: It is the Song of Miriam
It is only sung by angels
They sing a new song that no one else could learn
It is the Song of Solomon
questions.answer: They sing a new song that no one else could learn

questions.question: What did the prophet Jeremiah lament about the sound of their music during the Babylonian siege?
options: The joy of the heart had ceased, and the sound of the tambourine had stopped
questions.answer: The joy of the heart had ceased, and the sound of the tambourine had stopped

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the nature of the new song that is sung in the book of Revelation?
options: It is a song of redemption, praise to the Lamb, and victory
It is a song of the Law
It is a lament for the lost
questions.answer: It is a song of redemption, praise to the Lamb, and victory

questions.question: How did the early Christian hymn writers view their songs in relation to the Psalms?
options: They viewed them as New Covenant expressions of the themes in the Psalms
They believed their songs were inferior to the Psalms
questions.answer: They viewed them as New Covenant expressions of the themes in the Psalms

questions.question: What does the Bible say about the "music" and "dancing" that accompanied the golden calf worship?
options: It was an act of idolatry that angered God
questions.answer: It was an act of idolatry that angered God

questions.question: What are the "songs of degrees" (Songs of Ascents) and when were they typically sung?
options: Psalms 120-134, sung by pilgrims traveling to Jerusalem
questions.answer: Psalms 120-134, sung by pilgrims traveling to Jerusalem

questions.question: What is the theological meaning of praise as a "sacrifice" in the New Testament (Hebrews 13:15)?
options: It is the offering of our wealth
It is the offering of our lips (vocal praise)
It is the offering of our tears
questions.answer: It is the offering of our lips (vocal praise)

questions.question: What did the prophet Isaiah see the seraphim doing in terms of vocal praise in the Temple vision?
options: Crying out, "Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts"
Announcing the arrival of the Messiah
questions.answer: Crying out, "Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts"

questions.question: How did King Solomon organize the musicians and singers for the first Temple?
options: He gave the task to the priests
He confirmed the arrangement set up by his father, David
questions.answer: He confirmed the arrangement set up by his father, David

questions.question: What is the spiritual significance of the "new song" in Psalm 40, which the Psalmist sings after being delivered?
options: A promise of a new covenant
The joy and testimony of a person newly delivered or redeemed
questions.answer: The joy and testimony of a person newly delivered or redeemed

questions.question: What did Paul and Silas do at midnight in the jail that led to their liberation?
options: They were praying and singing hymns to God
questions.answer: They were praying and singing hymns to God

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew word "tehillah," often translated as "praise" (as in the title of the Book of Psalms)?
questions.answer: A spontaneous song or laudation

questions.question: What key worship principle did Jesus establish when he quoted Isaiah: "This people honors Me with their lips, but their heart is far from Me"?
options: The importance of the heart's genuine devotion
questions.answer: The importance of the heart's genuine devotion

questions.question: What does the book of Judges record about the music and songs of the women of Israel welcoming the victorious armies?
options: They went out with tambourines and dances
questions.answer: They went out with tambourines and dances

questions.question: What does the prophet Joel command the priests to do with their voices and actions during a time of famine and drought?
options: Lament, weep, mourn, and cry out to the LORD
questions.answer: Lament, weep, mourn, and cry out to the LORD

questions.question: What is the common distinction between the harp and the lyre in ancient Israel?
options: The Harp was for women, the Lyre for men
The Harp was generally large/free-standing; the Lyre was smaller/portable
The Harp was for war, the Lyre for peace
They were exactly the same instrument
questions.answer: The Harp was generally large/free-standing; the Lyre was smaller/portable

questions.question: What does the Psalmist mean by using his "glory" to sing praise to God (Psalm 30:12)?
options: His soul or inner being (the highest part of man)
questions.answer: His soul or inner being (the highest part of man)

questions.question: What did the prophet Haggai say about the lack of true worship and singing among the people after returning from exile?
options: He chastised them for focusing on their own houses while the Temple lay in ruins
He commanded them to stop singing
He said their singing was too professional
questions.answer: He chastised them for focusing on their own houses while the Temple lay in ruins

questions.question: What is the theological significance of the shofar sound on the Day of Atonement?
options: Announcement of the Year of Jubilee (release from debt and liberty)
questions.answer: Announcement of the Year of Jubilee (release from debt and liberty)

questions.question: What is the typical distinction between a psalm and a spiritual song in New Testament usage?
options: Psalms are slow, spiritual songs are fast
Psalm = OT adaptation; Spiritual Song = Contemporary, inspired song
Psalm = for groups, Spiritual Song = for solos
questions.answer: Psalm = OT adaptation; Spiritual Song = Contemporary, inspired song

questions.question: What does the prophet Malachi predict about the future worship of the Gentiles?
options: They will only worship in Jerusalem
God's name will be great among them, with a pure offering
questions.answer: God's name will be great among them, with a pure offering

questions.question: What is the significance of the "morning stars" singing together in the Book of Job?
options: They are the souls of the righteous
They signify the joy and praise of creation at the beginning of the world
questions.answer: They signify the joy and praise of creation at the beginning of the world

questions.question: What is the purpose of the frequent "bless the LORD" command in the Psalms?
options: To ask God for a blessing
To teach people how to sing
To invoke divine favor and express gratitude for God's goodness
questions.answer: To invoke divine favor and express gratitude for God's goodness

questions.question: What does the Bible record about the songs of the drunkards and the ungodly?
options: They are described as mocking and defiling
questions.answer: They are described as mocking and defiling

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew word "barach," often translated as "bless"?
options: To kneel down or bow in reverence
questions.answer: To kneel down or bow in reverence

questions.question: How does the Book of Nehemiah describe the joy of the people after reading the Law, in connection with the singers?
options: The joy of Jerusalem was great, and the singers had a prominent role
There was only weeping, no joy
The joy was subdued and quiet
questions.answer: The joy of Jerusalem was great, and the singers had a prominent role

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Hebrew word "shabach," often translated as "glory" or "triumph"?
options: To shout loudly or triumph (a loud, jubilant boast)
questions.answer: To shout loudly or triumph (a loud, jubilant boast)

questions.question: What does the Bible describe the angels doing in terms of vocal praise and worship in heaven?
options: They constantly cry out "Holy" and fall down to worship
questions.answer: They constantly cry out "Holy" and fall down to worship

questions.question: What specific musical skill did David have that first brought him to Saul's attention?
options: He was a great choir master
He was a skillful player on the lyre/harp
He was a master of the flute
questions.answer: He was a skillful player on the lyre/harp

questions.question: What does the prophet Zechariah say about the sound of the trumpets in the future deliverance of Israel?
options: The enemy will sound the trumpets
The LORD will blow the trumpet and go forth in the whirlwinds
Only the Levites will blow the trumpets
questions.answer: The LORD will blow the trumpet and go forth in the whirlwinds

questions.question: What does the phrase "Make a joyful noise" in the Psalms imply about the nature of praise?
options: That praise should be loud, enthusiastic, and unrestrained
That praise is only for the righteous
questions.answer: That praise should be loud, enthusiastic, and unrestrained
